zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Raleigh於1365年6月5日出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，她的父親Sir John Raleigh30歲，母親Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham30歲。她於1419年與Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP在英國戴文貝里 波梅洛易結婚。她去世於1436年在英國薩默塞特內特萊可姆享年71歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特內特萊可姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Raleigh于1365年6月5日出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，她的父亲Sir John Raleigh30岁，母亲Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham30岁。她于1419年与Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP在英国戴文貝里 波梅洛易结婚。她去世于1436年在英国薩默塞特內特萊可姆享年71岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特內特萊可姆。

nameMeanings.name: Raleigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Raleigh (House) in Pilton (Devon), recorded as Radeleia in 1086 and Rawlegh in 1535. The placename probably derives from Old English rǣge ‘roe deer’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The English explorer Sir Walter Raleigh (1554–1618) was born in Hayes Barton, Devon, into a family of Devon gentry. He was related to most of the West Country's important families, including that of Sir Francis Drake. His half-brother was the explorer Sir Humphrey Gilbert. In 1578 Raleigh was granted a patent to explore and colonize “unknown lands” in America. In the 1580s he was granted extensive estates in Munster (Ireland), in reward for his suppression of the Desmond Rebellions.

variants.name: Rawleigh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Orlo.
English: variant of   Raleigh  , reflecting a common pronunciation.

variants.name: Riley
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name chiefly from High Ryley in Accrington (Lancashire) and Riley in Kirkburton, which was in the manor of Wakefield (Yorkshire). In some cases the name may derive from Riley Farm in Eyam (Derbyshire), Royley in Royton (Lancashire), or Rylah in Scarcliffe (Derbyshire). The placenames all come from Old English rȳge ‘rye’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

Irish: from Ó Raghailligh ‘descendant of Raghailleach’, Old Irish Roghallach. This is the name of a chieftain family in Cavan, related to the O'Rourkes. In Anglicized form it has also been confused with Ó Raithile found in Munster, usually Anglicized as O'Rahilly or Rahilly . See O'Reilly .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Raleigh was born on 5 June 1365, in London, Middlesex, England, her father, Sir John Raleigh, was 30 and her mother, Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham, was 30. She married Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP in 1419, in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England. She died in 1436, in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Raleigh est née le 5 juin 1365, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Raleigh, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP en 1419, à Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Angleterre. Elle est décédée en 1436, à Nettlecombe, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Nettlecombe, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Raleigh am 5. Juni 1365 in London, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Raleigh 30 und ihre Mutter Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham 30. Sie hat 1419 in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP geheiratet. Sie starb 1436 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England und wurde in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Raleigh è nata il il 5 giugno 1365 nel London, Middlesex, England, sua padre, Sir John Raleigh, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP nel 1419, nel Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England. Morì nel 1436, nel Nettlecombe, Somerset, England, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolta a Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Raleighが1365年6月5日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父Sir John Raleighは30歳，母Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanhamは30歳であった。1419年にイギリスデボンバーリー ポメロイでSir Thomas Pomeroy MPと結婚した。彼女は1436年にイギリスソメルセトネットレコンベで71歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトネットレコンベにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Raleigh(이)가 1365년 6월 5일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Raleigh(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1419년 영국 데폰 베리 폼로이에서 Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1436년 영국 서머세트 네틀렉브에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 네틀렉브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Raleigh nasceu em 5 de junho de 1365, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Raleigh, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP em 1419, em Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Inglaterra. Ela faleceu em 1436, em Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 71 anos, e foi sepultada em Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Raleigh родилась 5 июня 1365 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Raleigh было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP в 1419 г. в местности бирри помирои, девон, Англия. Она умерла в 1436 г. в местности неттлекомб, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 71 год и была похоронена в местности неттлекомб, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Raleigh nació el 5 de junio de 1365, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Raleigh, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Pomeroy MP en 1419, en Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Inglaterra. Murió en 1436, en Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultada en Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
